summary: The midbrain is divided into tectum (dorsally), tegmentum (middle), and cerebral peduncles (ventrally).
The midbrain consists of only two parts: the tectum and the cerebral peduncles.
The midbrain is divided into basis pontis, tegmentum, and fourth ventricle floor.
Weber syndrome involves ipsilateral oculomotor nerve palsy with contralateral hemiplegia.
Weber syndrome involves ipsilateral trochlear nerve palsy with contralateral hemiplegia.
Weber syndrome involves contralateral oculomotor nerve palsy with ipsilateral hemiplegia.
The substantia nigra pars compacta produces dopamine, and its degeneration causes Parkinson's disease.
The substantia nigra pars reticulata produces dopamine, and its degeneration causes Parkinson's disease.
The red nucleus produces dopamine, and its degeneration causes Parkinson's disease.
The superior colliculus mediates visual reflexes, while the inferior colliculus is an auditory relay centre.
The inferior colliculus mediates visual reflexes, while the superior colliculus is an auditory relay.
Both superior and inferior colliculi are purely visual relay centres.
The cerebral aqueduct of Sylvius passes through the midbrain, connecting the third and fourth ventricles.
The cerebral aqueduct passes through the pons, connecting the third and fourth ventricles.
The lateral ventricles pass through the midbrain.
The red nucleus receives cerebellar input and gives rise to the rubrospinal tract, which facilitates flexor motor neurons.
The red nucleus gives rise to the corticospinal tract and facilitates extensor motor neurons.
The red nucleus is part of the basal ganglia and receives input from the caudate nucleus.
